human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authoritative public sources, scenario coverage, results, readiness review, deployment notes, marketing notes, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior for consistency with issue #149 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to existing COBOL and IBM Z application guidance, modernization, troubleshooting, and immediate subsystem dependencies.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, decay policy, and current status in manifest.yaml.

The package includes COBOL and mainframe-specific scenarios for compiler upgrades, CICS and IMS constraints, Db2 embedded SQL, API enablement, copybook interoperability, automation support, migration prioritization, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #149.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local reviewed COBOL and mainframe baseline.

Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment-note normalization, commercialization wording review, authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffolding review are delegated to #302 through #308 with local artifact fallback.
The package initializes safely with deferred shared-meta-agent retrieval and does not depend on delegated outputs to start or remain boundary-safe.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #149.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation and adaptation constraints, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes define the buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without broadening into adjacent specialties.
